questions.question: Which Notion plans allow you to use the Slack AI Connector?

options.text: Business or Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: The feature is only available on Business or Enterprise plans.

options.text: Free Plan
options.explanation: The free plan does not include the Slack AI Connector.

options.text: Personal Plan
options.explanation: Personal plans are for individual users and lack this integration.

options.text: Team Plan
options.explanation: Team plans are not mentioned as supporting the Slack AI Connector.

questions.question: Who must be both a Slack workspace owner and a Notion workspace owner to initially connect Slack to Notion AI?

options.text: A user who is both a Slack workspace owner and a Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: Only someone with ownership rights in both platforms can start the integration.

options.text: Any member of the Slack workspace
options.explanation: Regular members lack the required permissions to set up the connector.

options.text: A Notion admin without Slack permissions
options.explanation: Slack ownership is still required regardless of Notion admin status.

options.text: A guest user in either workspace
options.explanation: Guests cannot use the Slack AI Connector at all.

questions.question: During the setup process, where should you initiate the Slack AI connector?

options.text: In Notion (Settings → Notion AI)
options.explanation: The connector must be started from Notion; starting in Slack will not work.

options.text: Directly in Slack’s App Directory
options.explanation: Initiating in Slack will not trigger the required Notion flow.

options.text: From a third‑party integration platform
options.explanation: Only Notion’s native setup is supported.

options.text: Via the Notion mobile app
options.explanation: The documentation specifies using the web Settings, not the mobile app.

questions.question: After connecting Slack to Notion AI, how far back can Notion AI retrieve Slack messages?

options.text: Up to one year prior to the connection date
options.explanation: The connector indexes messages from the previous year once setup completes.

options.text: All historical messages, regardless of age
options.explanation: Only the last year is indexed; older messages are not searchable.

options.text: Only messages from the past six months
options.explanation: The timeframe is a full year, not six months.

options.text: Only messages created after the connection is made
options.explanation: Historical messages up to a year old are also included.

questions.question: Which type of Slack channel is explicitly excluded from being indexed by the Slack AI Connector?

options.text: Slack Connect channels that contain external users
options.explanation: External Slack Connect channels are never indexed for security reasons.

options.text: Public channels owned by the workspace
options.explanation: Public channels are indexed unless they are Slack Connect channels.

options.text: Private channels that you manually connect
options.explanation: Private channels can be connected by individual members.

options.text: Direct messages you choose to link
options.explanation: Direct messages can be linked by users; they are not automatically excluded.

questions.question: How long may it take for new messages in public Slack channels to become searchable via Notion AI?

options.text: About 30 minutes
options.explanation: New messages are typically indexed within roughly half an hour.

options.text: Immediately
options.explanation: There is a short indexing delay; it is not instant.

options.text: 24 hours
options.explanation: The delay is much shorter than a full day.

options.text: Up to 36 hours
options.explanation: 36 hours refers to the initial full‑channel indexing, not new messages.

questions.question: In what form does Notion store Slack content to enable fast search?

options.text: As embeddings in a vector database such as Turbopuffer
options.explanation: Slack messages are transformed into embeddings stored in a vetted vector database for efficient similarity search.

options.text: As plain‑text files on Notion’s servers
options.explanation: Plain text would not support the fast semantic search provided by embeddings.

options.text: In a relational SQL database
options.explanation: The architecture uses vector databases, not traditional relational tables.

options.text: Encrypted PDFs uploaded to Notion
options.explanation: PDFs are not used for indexing conversational Slack data.

questions.question: What happens to your Slack data if you disconnect the Slack AI Connector from Notion?

options.text: The data is deleted within less than a day and becomes unsearchable
options.explanation: Notion follows data minimization: content is removed quickly after disconnection.

options.text: The data is archived and remains searchable for 30 days
options.explanation: There is no archiving period; data is removed promptly.

options.text: The data is exported to a CSV file for your records
options.explanation: Export is not part of the disconnect process.

options.text: The data stays in Notion but is hidden from search
options.explanation: Deleted data is removed, not merely hidden.

questions.question: Are guest users in Notion or Slack able to use the Slack AI Connector?

options.text: No, guests are not supported
options.explanation: The integration explicitly excludes both Notion and Slack guests.

options.text: Yes, but only with read‑only access
options.explanation: Guests have no access to the connector at all.

options.text: Only Slack guests can use it
options.explanation: Neither type of guest is allowed.

options.text: Only Notion guests can use it
options.explanation: Both guest categories are excluded.

questions.question: If you are not a Slack workspace owner, how can you still get the Slack AI Connector set up for your workspace?

options.text: Request the integration, which notifies the Slack workspace owner or approved members
options.explanation: Non‑owners can send a request that alerts those with the necessary permissions.

options.text: Directly connect the workspace without any approval
options.explanation: Only owners or approved members can complete the connection.

options.text: Use a temporary admin account to gain ownership
options.explanation: Creating temporary admin rights is not part of the official process.

options.text: Wait for Notion to automatically add the connector after a week
options.explanation: The connector does not auto‑install; a request is required.
